prompt: A cinematic, low-angle photograph captured with a shallow depth of field inside a minimalist art gallery. The subject is a fine tweed wool suit displayed on a stylized, articulated mannequin. The mannequin is contorted into a bizarre, extremely awkward and stationary posture: sitting on a concrete plinth, bent double, holding a large river stone against its right knee, while its left leg is crossed strangely behind it. The suit fabric is physically molded and rigidified around this exact, contorted posture. Every wrinkle, crease, and awkward contour is permanent, Matching the 'noise' of the bizarre pose. Stitches are pulled extremely taut around the shoulders and elbows, visualizing the inability to move or generalize back to a standard upright position. Volumetric lighting from spotlights, hyper-realistic textures. --ar 16:9 --v 6.1 --no charts, graphs, text, floating labels
metaphor: A carved figure rigidified into a bizarre contortion, visualizing a model that has learned specific training noise so perfectly it cannot move into any general pose.
